	buffering)
subprocess
PIPErz

_procr&

)	r 
stdin_wr
_start
_UnixSubprocessTransport._startN)
Abstract base class for monitoring child processes.
    Objects derived from this class monitor a collection of subprocesses and
    report their termination or interruption by a signal.
    New callbacks are registered with .add_child_handler(). Starting a new
    process must be done within a 'with' block to allow the watcher to suspend
    its activity until the new process if fully registered (this is needed to
    prevent a race condition in some implementations).
    Example:
        with watcher:
            proc = subprocess.Popen("sleep 1")
            watcher.add_child_handler(proc.pid, callback)
    Notes:
        Implementations of this class must be thread-safe.
        Since child watcher objects may catch the SIGCHLD signal and call
        waitpid(-1), there should be only one active object per process.
    c
Register a new child handler.
        Arrange for callback(pid, returncode, *args) to be called when
        process 'pid' terminates. Specifying another callback for the same
        process replaces the previous handler.
        Note: callback() must be thread-safe.
        N
NotImplementedError
	z&AbstractChildWatcher.add_child_handlerc
Removes the handler for process 'pid'.
        The function returns True if the handler was successfully removed,
        False if there was nothing to remove.Nr
remove_child_handlerD
z)AbstractChildWatcher.remove_child_handlerc
Attach the watcher to an event loop.
        If the watcher was previously attached to an event loop, then it is
        first detached before attaching to the new loop.
        Note: loop may be None.
        Nr
attach_loopL
z AbstractChildWatcher.attach_loopc
zlClose the watcher.
        This must be called to make sure that any underlying resource is freed.
        Nr
AbstractChildWatcher.closec
Return ``True`` if the watcher is active and is used by the event loop.
        Return True if the watcher is installed and ready to handle process exit
        notifications.
        Nr
AbstractChildWatcher.is_activec
zdEnter the watcher's context and allow starting new processes
        This function must return selfNr
	__enter__f
AbstractChildWatcher.__enter__c
Exit the watcher's contextNr
__exit__l
AbstractChildWatcher.__exit__N)
Child watcher implementation using Linux's pid file descriptors.
    This child watcher polls process file descriptors (pidfds) to await child
    process termination. In some respects, PidfdChildWatcher is a "Goldilocks"
    child watcher implementation. It doesn't require signals or threads, doesn't
    interfere with any processes launched outside the event loop, and scales
    linearly with the number of subprocesses launched by the event loop. The
    main disadvantage is that pidfds are specific to Linux, and only work on
    recent (5.3+) kernels.
    c
_callbacksr
PidfdChildWatcher.__init__c
PidfdChildWatcher.__enter__c
exc_type
	exc_value
exc_tracebackr
PidfdChildWatcher.__exit__c
is_runningr
PidfdChildWatcher.is_activec
PidfdChildWatcher.closec
NzCA loop is being detached from a child watcher with pending handlers)

AbstractEventLoopr
SIGCHLDrI
	_sig_chldr=
BaseChildWatcher.attach_loopc
$Unknown exception in SIGCHLD handler)
BaseChildWatcher._sig_chldN)
'Safe' child watcher implementation.
    This implementation avoids disrupting other code spawning processes by
    polling explicitly each process in the SIGCHLD handler instead of calling
    os.waitpid(-1).
    This is a safe solution but it has a significant overhead when handling a
    big number of children (O(n) each time SIGCHLD is raised)
    c
SafeChildWatcher.closec
SafeChildWatcher.__enter__c
SafeChildWatcher.__exit__c
z"SafeChildWatcher.add_child_handlerc
z%SafeChildWatcher.remove_child_handlerc
z SafeChildWatcher._do_waitpid_allc
8Unknown child process pid %d, will report returncode 255
$process %s exited with returncode %s
'Child watcher got an unexpected pid: %rTr
WNOHANGr3
SafeChildWatcher._do_waitpid)
'Fast' child watcher implementation.
    This implementation reaps every terminated processes by calling
    os.waitpid(-1) directly, possibly breaking other code spawning processes
    and waiting for their termination.
    There is no noticeable overhead when handling a big number of children
    (O(1) each time a child terminates).
    c
	threadingZ
Lock
_lock
_zombies
_forksr
FastChildWatcher.__init__c
FastChildWatcher.closec
FastChildWatcher.__enter__c
z5Caught subprocesses termination from unknown pids: %s)
collateral_victimsr
FastChildWatcher.__exit__c
Must use the context manager)
z"FastChildWatcher.add_child_handlerc
z%FastChildWatcher.remove_child_handlerc
z,unknown process %s exited with returncode %srE
z8Caught subprocess termination from unknown pid: %d -> %d)
z FastChildWatcher._do_waitpid_all)
A watcher that doesn't require running loop in the main thread.
    This implementation registers a SIGCHLD signal handler on
    instantiation (which may conflict with other code that
    install own handler for this signal).
    The solution is safe but it has a significant overhead when
    handling a big number of processes (*O(n)* each time a
    SIGCHLD is received).
    c
_saved_sighandlerr
MultiLoopChildWatcher.__init__c
MultiLoopChildWatcher.is_activec

NzaPrevious SIGCHLD handler was set by non-Python code, restore to default handler on watcher close.F)
z!MultiLoopChildWatcher.attach_loopc
z%MultiLoopChildWatcher._do_waitpid_allc

%Loop %r that handles pid %r is closedrE
	is_closedr
)	r 
Z	debug_logr
z!MultiLoopChildWatcher._do_waitpidc
MultiLoopChildWatcher._sig_chldN)
Threaded child watcher implementation.
    The watcher uses a thread per process
    for waiting for the process finish.
    It doesn't require subscription on POSIX signal
    but a thread creation is not free.
    The watcher has O(1) complexity, its performance doesn't depend
    on amount of spawn processes.
    c
	itertoolsr
_pid_counter
_threadsr
ThreadedChildWatcher.__init__c
ThreadedChildWatcher.is_activec
_join_threadsr

_UnixDefaultEventLoopPolicyz:UNIX event loop policy with a watcher for child processes.c

_init_watcher
z)_UnixDefaultEventLoopPolicy._init_watcherc
Set the event loop.
        As a side effect, if a child watcher was set before, then calling
        .set_event_loop() from the main thread will call .attach_loop(loop) on
        the child watcher.
        N)

z~Get the watcher for child processes.
        If not yet set, a ThreadedChildWatcher object is automatically created.
        N)
